Penalties are their own thing. This is more akin to either offside or a close call with a goal the ref initially thinks isn't in. For offside, they'll allow the play to go on and check after and for goals either the score judge will confirm via a buzzer or they'll go back and check once the play is blown dead. In this situation it's perfectly logical to allow the play to continue and review it after

That’s a very big IF. Efficiency always comes down with more shots taken so you can’t just assume that if he takes 6 more 3s a game that he’d sustain the same efficiency. Curry is a career 42,5% 3pt shooter while taking 9,2 attempts a game while Reggie is a career 39,5% 3pt shooter with only 4,7 attempts a game. That’s night and day difference and there really is no argument for Reggie to be a better shooter than Curry. A much higher percentage of Reggie’s shots were open than Curry’s so that’s also something to keep in mind.

I don’t believe they give them a cut of ad revenue. They pay the leagues a contractually-negotiated annual fee for broadcast rights, and then the network (ESPN in this case), uses advertising revenue (as well as additional revenue sources) to recoup the money they spent on the broadcast rights.
